title: Mesh routing change caused distributed timeout chain across checkout path
summary: Checkout success collapsed after a distributed timeout chain spread across pricing, inventory, and payment services. The incident looked at first like a payment gateway degradation or a single overloaded service, but the real RCA requires following the timeline of service-mesh retries, timeout inflation, queue backpressure, and a routing policy rollout that changed cross-zone call paths.
primary_hypotheses: service mesh retry and timeout amplification
operational_context: A zone-aware routing policy rollout at 19:02Z changed fallback behavior when local endpoints were scarce.
One inventory node pool in us-east-1b was already running at reduced spare capacity due to maintenance.
